In which of the following systems is the power of the component units more than that of the central government?
solution: In a confederal system, the component units (such as states or regions) possess more authority than the central government. This arrangement allows the constituent entities to retain their sovereignty and delegate only specific, limited powers to the central authority, often related to common interests like defense or foreign affairs. Consequently, the central government operates at the behest of the component units and lacks independent authority.

option.b: existence of administrative tribunals and special immunities

option.d: inability of the press to discharge its responsibilities

solution: The rule of law is based on the principle that every individual and institution is subject to the same laws, without exceptions. These exceptions can erode public trust in the legal system by undermining the notion that the law is applied uniformly and fairly.

question: Socialism is a mode of production based on

option.a: state state ownership of the means of production
option.b: collective ownership of the means of production
option.c: national ownership of the means of productions
option.d: mixed ownership of means of production

solution: Socialism is primarily defined by the principle that the means of production—such as factories, resources, and infrastructure—are owned collectively by the community rather than by private individuals. This collective or communal ownership is intended to ensure that production and the benefits derived from it serve the common good, rather than generating profit for private owners. While many socialist states have implemented state ownership as a means of achieving collective control, the core concept remains the collective (or community) ownership of production resources.

question: The ________ as a new policy direction introduced by the French government that was aimed at granting more autonomy to its colonies under pressure from independence movements.

question: The Igbo political system was based on
solution: The Igbo pre-colonial political system was fundamentally decentralized and based on kinship structures, with the Umunna serving as the primary unit of political organization. Each Umunna was led by the eldest male, known as the Diokpala, who, along with other elders, played a significant role in decision-making and governance within the community. This structure ensured that authority was distributed among various family heads, fostering a republican and egalitarian society

solution:
One of the organizational weaknesses of the Organization of Petroleum Exporting Countries is its inability to punish erring members.

Some member countries exceed the production quotas while some do not mind the fixed prices. Such defaulters always go unpunished.

question: Unicameral legislature is a common feature of
solution: A unicameral legislature (single legislative chamber) is most commonly associated with unitary systems of government. In unitary states, power is centralized, and subnational units (if they exist) derive authority from the central government. A single legislative body aligns with this simplicity and centralized decision-making.

question: Which of the following is a function of the Public Complaints Commission?
solution: Public commissions are responsible for conducting investigations and enquiries. They are established to investigate matters of public concern and to make recommendations based on their findings. They do not implement foreign policies, enforce criminal laws, or manage public transportation.

question: Nigeria spearheaded the formation of ECOWAS during the regime of
solution: Nigeria played a pivotal role in the formation of the Economic Community of West African States (ECOWAS) during the regime of General Yakubu Gowon. In collaboration with Togolese President Gnassingbé Eyadéma, Gowon initiated efforts to promote economic integration in West Africa. Their joint diplomatic missions across the region culminated in the signing of the Treaty of Lagos on May 28, 1975, officially establishing ECOWAS. ​

question: The right of all eligible citizens to vote and be voted for is referred to as
solution:
The right of all eligible citizens to vote and be voted for is referred to as universal suffrage.

Universal suffrage gives voting right to all qualified citizens. But restricted suffrage or restricted franchise restricts voting rights to specified citizens based on age, sex, property, wealth, race or education.

question: Nigeria is a major financier of which of the following?
solution: Nigeria is a major financier of the African Development Bank (AFDB), which is the leading financial institution in Africa, providing funding for development projects across the continent. Nigeria is one of the largest shareholders and a major contributor to the bank’s resources.

question: Southern Nigeria was divided into Eastern and Western provinces for administrative purposes in _________
solution:
In 1939, Southern Nigeria was officially divided into the Eastern and Western Provinces by Governor Bernard Bourdillon for administrative convenience.

Before this, Southern Nigeria was administered as a single entity after the 1914 amalgamation of the Northern and Southern Protectorates.
The division allowed for better governance and set the foundation for the later creation of Nigeria’s regions in 1954.

question: Which of the following is not relevant to communism
The Ibadan Conference of 1950 was convened to discuss the provisions of the
solution:
The Ibadan Conference of 1950 was convened to discuss the provisions of the Macpherson Constitution.

Sir John Macphereson became Government of Nigeria in 1948. He then took immediate steps to review Sir Arthur Richard's Constitution of 1946 and fashioned a new constitution which came into being in 1951. To avoid the mistakes of his predecessor, Sir Arthur Richard, on the issue of consultation, the draft of Macpherson Constitution was seriously debated at the village district, provincial and regional level and later on at a General Conference held in Ibadan in the month of January 1950. The outcome of these constitutional Conferences formed the basis of the Macpherson constitution of 1951.

question: The major factor militating against the efficient operation of electoral commissions in Nigeria is
n the Presidential System of Government, the president is elected to office by
solution: In a Presidential System of Government, the president is elected to office by the people through direct elections. question: On 1 October 1995, Abacha announced a three-year program for return to civilian rule. Parties were allowed to form in July 1998. Which of the following parties was not registered by the Provisional Ruling Council?

question: The power to nominate members of the judiciary at the federal level under the 1999 Constitution is vested in the
solution: Under Nigeria’s 1999 Constitution, the National Judicial Council (NJC) is vested with the authority to nominate members of the federal judiciary for appointment by the President. This process ensures judicial independence and adherence to constitutional standards.

question: Lack of separation of power is a feature of
solution: Separation of power is the division of powers and functions among the three arms of government - the legislature, executive and judiciary. This is essential to prevent the abuse of power when it is concentrated in the hands of a single authority. In a presidential system, there is a clear separation of power. In a cabinet system, there is still separation of power although, it is not as clear (this is because the legislature and executive arms are fused). However, in a military government, power is concentrated in the hands of the military leader. He has the power to make laws through the promulgation of decrees; to execute the law; as well as to punish law breakers. In this case, there is a lack of separation of power.
